key_points: A shirt passes through many stages before reaching the market
Cotton farmers grow cotton which is used as raw material for cloth
Traders buy cotton from farmers and sell it to factories
Workers in factories spin yarn, weave cloth, and stitch shirts
Large business people and brands earn the highest profit in the market chain
examples: Cotton farmers sell cotton to traders at low prices
Garment workers stitch shirts in factories for daily wages
Export companies sell shirts in international markets
Branded companies sell shirts in malls and big stores
Middlemen like traders connect farmers, factories, and retailers
formulas: Cotton Farmer → Trader → Factory → Garment Worker → Retail Shop → Customer
Production Chain → Different people involved in producing and selling a product
Profit Distribution → Larger companies usually earn more than workers
exam_tips: Understand the production chain of a shirt from cotton to market
Remember the roles of farmers, traders, factory workers, and retailers
Focus on how profits are distributed among different people
Learn examples showing inequality in earnings between workers and companies
